results.title: Type 2 Diabetes: Management and Treatment
results.snippet: Metformin is recommended as the first-line medication for type 2 diabetes, alongside diet and exercise. Newer options include GLP-1 receptor agonists and SGLT2 inhibitors, which also offer cardiovascular benefits.

results.title: Standards of Care in Diabetes
results.snippet: Treatment goals focus on glycemic control (target HbA1c often below 7%), blood pressure, and lipid management. Lifestyle modification remains foundational across all stages.

results.title: GLP-1 Receptor Agonists in Type 2 Diabetes
results.snippet: GLP-1 agonists such as semaglutide improve glucose control and support weight loss, and are increasingly used when metformin alone is insufficient.
